Title: Identification by Tn-seq of Dickeya dadantii genes required for survival in chicory plants

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High c-di-GMP is associated with reduced virulence during plant infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of gcpA increases bacterial fitness and hypervirulence in chicory, consistent with reduced c-di-GMP favoring infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gcpA encodes a GGDEF protein implicated in c-di-GMP synthesis. 2. gcpA mutants were over-represented in chicory in Tn-seq. 3. In-frame deletion confirmed hypervirulence/competitive advantage in planta.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion mutant; chicory competition assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Chicory leaf infection at high humidity, 30°C
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states opposite phenotype to ecpC mutants indicates that overproduction of c-di-GMP could reduce D. dadantii virulence.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bacterial fitness and disease development in plants
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation reduces FliC glycosylation and decreases bacterial virulence in celery; associated with growth defect in chicory
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dda3937_03424 encodes a predicted O-linked N-acetylglucosamine transferase in the fliA-fliC region. 2. Mutation lowered apparent and measured molecular mass of FliC, consistent with loss of glycosylation. 3. Glycosylation mutant showed reduced maceration on celery and fitness defect in chicory Tn-seq.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; mutant construction (A4277); SDS-PAGE; MALDI-MS; celery virulence assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plant infection in chicory and celery at high humidity, 30°C
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of glycosylation did not affect motility, indicating specific contribution to virulence rather than flagellar assembly.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antibacterial target to reduce soft-rot virulence

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes bacterial fitness during plant infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted glycosyltransferase required for full FliC glycosylation; mutation decreased FliC molecular weight
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dda3937_03419 is a predicted nucleotide diphospho sugar transferase in the fliA-fliC cluster. 2. Mutation reduced FliC mass, supporting a role in flagellin glycosylation. 3. Gene is part of a cluster important for fitness in chicory.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Together with Dda3937_03424, suggests multiple glycosylation with a disaccharide on FliC.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in synthesis of carbamoyl phosphate from glutamine, bicarbonate and ATP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key entry step for UMP biosynthesis in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for growth and survival in chicory; deletion mutant unable to survive competitively in plant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. carA was a strong growth-defect gene in chicory by Tn-seq. 2. UMP biosynthetic pathway was identified as essential in planta. 3. In-frame deletion confirmed inability to survive competitively in chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes chicory is poor in pyrimidine precursors, making de novo synthesis essential.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Bacteria-specific nucleotide biosynthesis step proposed as target for specific inhibitors to prevent infection

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalytic large subunit of carbamoyl-phosphate synthase producing carbamoyl phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key entry step for UMP biosynthesis in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. carB was a significant growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Supports importance of de novo pyrimidine biosynthesis in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pyrB is a significant growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Supports essential role of UMP biosynthesis in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pyrC was identified as a growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Part of the UMP pathway required in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for survival in chicory; deletion mutant unable to survive competitively in plant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pyrE was a strong growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Deletion mutant was strongly outcompeted by WT in planta. 3. Confirms UMP biosynthesis is essential in chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for survival in chicory; deletion mutant unable to survive competitively in plant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. purF was a strong growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Purine biosynthesis was essential for bacterial multiplication in planta. 3. In-frame deletion mutant was strongly impaired in chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes low nucleic acid availability in chicory.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for survival in chicory; deletion mutant unable to survive competitively in plant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. purL showed one of the strongest fitness defects in chicory. 2. In-frame deletion confirmed severe defect in planta. 3. Confirms de novo IMP synthesis is essential during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial nucleic acid synthesis and survival in planta
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes IMP to xanthosine monophosphate in guanine nucleotide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for survival in chicory; deletion mutant severely impaired in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. guaB had a strong negative fitness score in chicory. 2. In-frame deletion mutant was strongly outcompeted by WT. 3. Indicates guanine nucleotide biosynthesis is limiting in the plant environment.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly notes strong log2FC for guaB.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial protein synthesis and growth in amino-acid-poor plant tissue
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Branched-chain amino acid biosynthesis / leucine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses acetyl-CoA with 2-oxoisovalerate to initiate leucine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for chicory colonization; deletion mutant strongly impaired, and phenotype complemented by leucine addition
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. leuA was a growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. In-frame deletion showed strong competitive defect. 3. Exogenous leucine rescued the auxotroph in planta, showing leucine scarcity in chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay; in planta amino acid complementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Chicory infection; leucine supplementation in complementation assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper concludes certain amino acids are scarce in chicory tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Bacterial amino acid biosynthetic enzymes proposed as targets for specific inhibitors

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial growth in sulfur-amino-acid-poor plant tissue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for chicory infection; auxotrophic defect rescued by amino acid supplementation in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. metB was a growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Mutant showed reduced competitiveness in planta. 3. Sulfur amino acid supplementation complemented the defect, supporting low amino acid availability in chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; chicory competition assay; in planta amino acid complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses sulfur-containing amino acids as limiting in chicory.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial growth in sulfur-amino-acid-poor plant tissue
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Sulfite reductase NADPH flavoprotein alpha component
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces sulfite to sulfide for sulfur assimilation and cysteine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for growth in chicory; deletion mutant impaired in competition assays and auxotrophy rescued by supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cysJ was a growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. Deletion mutant was defective in planta. 3. Sulfur amino acid supplementation complemented the auxotrophic phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay; in planta amino acid complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper groups cysIJQ as important for sulfur amino acid synthesis in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial protein synthesis and growth in lysine-poor plant tissue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for chicory survival; deletion mutant strongly impaired and auxotrophy could be complemented in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lysA was a significant growth-defect gene in chicory. 2. In-frame deletion mutant was strongly outcompeted by WT. 3. Lysine supplementation rescued the defect, indicating lysine scarcity in chicory.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay; in planta amino acid complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the most severe auxotrophic defects validated experimentally.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Virulence factor production and motility regulation affecting plant tissue colonization
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation of virulence via modulation of FlhDC transcriptional activity
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FlhDC-controlled motility and extracellular enzyme production pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent negative controller of plant infection fitness
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through modulation of FlhCD transcriptional activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion causes hypervirulence and overgrowth in chicory
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rsmC mutants were over-represented in chicory in Tn-seq. 2. In-frame deletion confirmed enhanced competitiveness in planta. 3. Paper interprets RsmC as a negative regulator of infection, likely through FlhDC.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized in Pectobacterium carotovorum as controlling motility and extracellular enzymes.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Virulence and motility regulation during plant colonization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Could activate flhDC expression in D. dadantii
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: supports fitness during chicory infection by promoting virulence regulatory cascade
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss impairs bacterial fitness in chicory
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hdfR was a growth-defect regulatory gene in chicory. 2. Deletion mutant was outcompeted by WT in planta. 3. Authors propose HdfR may act as an activator of flhDC expression in D. dadantii.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulatory connection is proposed by analogy to E. coli and Salmonella studies.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: adjacent genes; additional unknown targets inferred
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major regulator required for chicory infection though direct metabolic target remains unknown
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to fitness in chicory; mutation causes growth defect in planta
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LfcR was identified as important for chicory infection. 2. It is a LacI-family repressor previously implicated in infection of multiple hosts. 3. Its known adjacent targets did not explain the phenotype, implying undiscovered targets.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes surprise that adjacent repressed genes were not themselves implicated in chicory infection.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-virulence target, but downstream targets need clarification

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FlhDC flagellar master regulator complex component
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flagellar motility and virulence factor synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: flagellar genes; virulence factor synthesis pathways; type III secretion system (as described in cited/related work and paper discussion)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator decisive for motility-dependent fitness in planta
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: RsmC and HdfR are discussed as regulators affecting flhDC expression/activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in FlhDC master regulator complex with FlhD
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fitness in chicory; deletion mutant outcompeted by WT
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. flhC was identified as important for chicory infection. 2. Authors discuss FlhDC as master regulator of flagella and virulence factors. 3. In-frame flhDC mutant showed reduced competitiveness in planta.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Tn-seq; in-frame deletion; chicory competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper also notes all genes required for flagella synthesis and chemotaxis confer benefit in planta.
